name: Prehistoric Era
description: The period of human history before the invention of writing systems, encompassing the development of stone tools, fire, language, and early social structures.

name: Ancient Egypt
description: One of the world's earliest and longest-lasting civilizations, centered on the Nile River, characterized by pharaonic rule, monumental architecture, and hieroglyphic writing.

name: Bronze Age
description: Period characterized by the widespread use of bronze for tools and weapons, the rise of early states and cities, and the development of writing systems across multiple regions.

name: Classical Greece
description: The golden age of ancient Greek culture, marked by the emergence of democracy, philosophy, drama, and advances in mathematics and science centered in Athens and other city-states.

name: Roman Republic
description: The republican phase of ancient Rome, governed by a senate and elected consuls, during which Rome expanded to dominate the Mediterranean world through military conquest and political organization.

name: Roman Empire
description: The imperial phase of Rome under emperors, encompassing the height of Roman power, the Pax Romana, the spread of Christianity, and eventual decline and fall in the West.

name: Gupta Empire
description: Often called the Golden Age of India, the Gupta period saw remarkable achievements in science, mathematics, astronomy, literature, and philosophy under a centralized Hindu empire.

name: Medieval Europe
description: The period between the fall of the Western Roman Empire and the Renaissance, characterized by feudalism, the dominance of the Catholic Church, the Crusades, Gothic architecture, and the Black Death.

name: Islamic Golden Age
description: A period of cultural, economic, and scientific flourishing in the Islamic world under the Abbasid Caliphate, during which scholars made major contributions to mathematics, astronomy, medicine, and philosophy.

name: Tang and Song Dynasties
description: Two successive Chinese dynasties representing a high point of Chinese civilization, notable for printing, gunpowder, the compass, porcelain, poetry, and a flourishing silk road trade.

name: Mongol Empire
description: The largest contiguous land empire in history, founded by Genghis Khan, which connected East and West through the Pax Mongolica, enabling unprecedented trade and cultural exchange along the Silk Road.

name: Renaissance
description: A cultural and intellectual movement originating in Italy that revived classical Greek and Roman learning, produced extraordinary art and literature, and laid the groundwork for modern science and humanism.

name: Age of Exploration
description: A period of extensive overseas exploration by European powers, connecting the Americas, Africa, Asia, and Europe and initiating a global exchange of goods, peoples, diseases, and ideas known as the Columbian Exchange.

name: Scientific Revolution
description: A fundamental transformation in scientific thought that established the heliocentric model of the solar system, the laws of motion and gravitation, and the experimental method as the foundation of modern science.

name: Enlightenment
description: An intellectual and philosophical movement emphasizing reason, individualism, liberty, and skepticism of traditional authority, laying the ideological groundwork for modern democracy, human rights, and secular governance.

name: Industrial Revolution
description: Period of transition from agrarian to manufacturing economies, beginning in Britain, characterized by the rise of factories, steam power, railways, and mass urbanization.

name: Age of Revolutions
description: A period of interconnected political upheavals across the Atlantic world and beyond, including the American and French Revolutions, Haitian independence, and the spread of liberal nationalism across Europe and Latin America.

name: Victorian Era
description: The reign of Queen Victoria marked by British imperial expansion, rapid technological innovation, social reform movements, and profound cultural change including the rise of the novel and Darwin's theory of evolution.

name: Meiji Restoration
description: Japan's rapid transformation from a feudal society to a modern industrialized nation under Emperor Meiji, through selective adoption of Western technology, institutions, and military organization.

name: World War Era
description: Three decades of global conflict including the First and Second World Wars, the Great Depression, the rise of totalitarianism, the Holocaust, and the use of nuclear weapons, reshaping the world political order.

name: Cold War
description: A geopolitical and ideological struggle between the United States and the Soviet Union and their respective allies, characterized by nuclear deterrence, proxy wars, the space race, and decolonization.

name: Postcolonial Era
description: The period of rapid decolonization in Africa and Asia following World War II, as dozens of new nations gained independence from European empires, forming the foundation of the modern developing world.

name: Information Age
description: A period of rapid technological advancement driven by the development of personal computers, the internet, and digital communications, fundamentally transforming economies, culture, and daily life worldwide.

name: Anthropocene
description: The proposed geological epoch beginning around the mid-20th century, defined by dominant human impact on Earth's geology, climate, and ecosystems, including climate change, mass extinction, and global-scale pollution.

name: AI Era
description: A transformative period defined by the rapid development of artificial intelligence, machine learning, and large language models, reshaping industries, scientific research, and society at an unprecedented pace.
